description:
KNOWN LIMITATION introduced as a scope decision in bl-c0c6 (state_remote). cmd_sync_run (src/commands/sync.rs) gates ALL of sync_with_remote — including the state-branch leg — behind git_has_remote(&store.root, <code remote, default origin>). So a hub-linked client that has a reachable state_remote but NO code 'origin' remote (a repo with code but no code remote, only a task hub) silently skips syncing balls/tasks on a plain 'bl sync'.

Not data-losing and narrow: bl claim/review/close with require_remote_on_* and bl remaster already round-trip the state branch against state_remote regardless (claim_sync uses policy::state_remote_opt). The recommended hub topology has both an origin and a hub remote, so the common path is unaffected and byte-identical. Only the standalone 'bl sync' in the code-remote-absent topology is affected.

FIX: decouple the two legs' gating in cmd_sync_run/sync_with_remote — gate the state-branch leg on the resolved state_remote existing (git_has_remote(root, state_remote)) and the main leg on the code remote, instead of one outer code-remote gate. Must stay byte-identical when state_remote is unset/==origin (conformance: every git invocation unchanged for a single-repo setup; mirror the bl-c0c6 state_remote.rs tests). Add an integration test: hub-only client (no origin) → bl sync pushes balls/tasks to the hub.
title: bl sync: sync the state branch even when the code remote is absent
